text: The surgeon uses a laparoscopic camera and three additional ports below the chest to access the surgical site. The artery is elevated, creating a tunnel-like space beneath it to expose the yellow bile duct and associated blood vessels. Clips are applied to the vessels, and the surrounding yellow tissue is cleaned to improve visibility.
The surgeon carefully separates the gallbladder from the liver bed, detaching it from its resting position within the liver tissue.
